domain: Macrotermes termite mound passive ventilation — central chimney + base pores; warm metabolic air rises by convection, cool air drawn in; no active fanning required.
specific_mechanism: Density-gradient-driven passive convection: heat from metabolic + diurnal oscillation drives warm air up through chimney; cooler dense air drawn in via lateral pores. Geometry built stigmergically.
llm_application: MOUND-CONVECT: dual-path context-gating. Compute per-token activation-temperature (norm-based) at each layer. Tokens above threshold are routed via 'chimney' fast-path to next layer's residual stream; tokens below threshold cycle into a 'lateral cool-air reservoir' (low-rank skip-storage). Reservoir tokens slowly mix back into the stream over K layers. Routing decision is purely activation-magnitude — no learned router parameter.
novelty_claim: MoD (Mixture-of-Depths) learns a per-token routing decision. Attention-sink uses fixed anchor tokens. Token pruning ranks by accumulated attention. None route by passive activation-magnitude convection with dual-path (chimney + lateral reservoir) architecture and slow-mix reservoir recycling.
